page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include any local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge sits primarily in the lower tiers for modern compute tasks. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench rank in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, the instance achieves average results in Passmark CPU scores and extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, though small-block cached read bandwidth and database operations are weak. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but poor for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge offers a dedicated CPU allocation on legacy Intel Xeon hardware. The lack of bundled local storage and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position it as a budget-conscious option for workloads that require dedicated cores but do not demand high-performance memory throughput or modern instruction sets like AVX-512 VNNI.

This server type is suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, software compilation, and small-scale model inference. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput cryptography, or large-scale data processing due to its lower memory-per-core ratio and weak single-core performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows weak single-core and multi-core performance, though it maintains average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark CPU extended instruction tests. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.sn1ne.2xlarge is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, basic software compilation, and small-scale web serving where dedicated physical cores are required without the need for high-speed local storage or massive memory capacity.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute instance with a one-to-two processor-to-memory ratio, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
